title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics · Execution Trace: Feed Go Content Revisions
summary: Every poll inserts duplicates, title edits create new posts, reused GUIDs overwrite unrelated content, disappeared entries are deleted immediately, and revision causes vanish. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go content revisions increment in idempotent versioned entry ingestion model. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.
Insert new, ignore unchanged, version changed, and quarantine conflicting entries atomically under unique constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Content Revisions · Theory Feed Go Content Revisions
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7626 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
